name: Schofield Barracks
tagline: Home of the 25th Infantry Division, the 'Tropic Lightning' division, in the heart of Oahu.
_note: Army Hawaii (USAG Hawaii), central Oahu. Marked OCONUS (Hawaii PCS is an overseas-style move) but BAH-rated: MHA HI408 (Honolulu County). Facts verified from official sources (home.army.mil/hawaii, islandpalmcommunities.com, tripler.tricare.mil, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Housing via Island Palm Communities (Lendlease). No DoDEA in Hawaii (Hawaii State DOE). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), representative off-base ZIP 96789 (Mililani).

data.Also part of: U.S. Army Garrison Hawaii (with Wheeler, Fort Shafter)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Schofield Barracks is the largest Army installation in Hawaii and has been the home of the 25th Infantry Division, the 'Tropic Lightning' division, since 1941. It sits on the central plateau of Oahu near Wahiawa.
It anchors U.S. Army Garrison Hawaii, which also includes Wheeler Army Airfield, Fort Shafter (the Army's Pacific headquarters), and several smaller posts across the island.

sections.title: An Island Assignment: Central Oahu Army Life

data.subtitle: Hawaii is paradise and a logistical puzzle. BAH is high but the cost of living is higher, and from Schofield's central-Oahu perch the commute and climate trade-offs differ from the town-side bases.
intro: A Hawaii tour is a true OCONUS-style move even though it's a U.S. state: household goods ship by sea, you usually bring one vehicle at government expense, and imported goods cost more. On-base housing is often the best value against high island rents.

Schofield sits on the cooler, greener central plateau. Nearby Mililani and Wahiawa are convenient, while commuting to Honolulu means the famously congested H-1 and H-2 freeways, so many families prioritize living close to post.

recommendations.advice: Island Palm Communities (Lendlease) runs the Army's Oahu housing; get on the list early, since on-base often beats high island rents and includes utilities.
Mililani and Wahiawa sit near Schofield with shorter commutes and are popular with Army families.
Arrange your one-vehicle shipment, expect a sea shipment for household goods, and budget for a higher cost of living than the BAH suggests.
H-1 and H-2 traffic into Honolulu is heavy; living near post saves real time day to day.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official USAG Hawaii site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: On-post family housing across the Army's Oahu installations is privatized, operated by Island Palm Communities (Lendlease), with neighborhoods at Schofield, Wheeler, Helemano, Fort Shafter, and more.
Given high island rents, on-base is often the best value, so get on the list early. Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA HI408 (Honolulu County)

sections.title: Schools
data: Hawaii runs a single statewide public school system (the Hawaii State Department of Education), so there is no separate local district and no DoDEA here; your school is assigned by your home address.
Confirm the specific school for any neighborhood you are considering, and ask the School Liaison Office about geographic exceptions if needed.

data.Hospital: Tripler Army Medical Center (serves all services in Hawaii)
data.Care: Tripler is the major inpatient and specialty hospital for the Pacific region

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves. In Hawaii it leans toward renting or living on base for most families, because home prices are among the highest in the country and tours are usually a fixed length.
rent: Your tour is a standard length and your orders feel fixed.
Island home prices are out of reach for your budget, which is common.
On-base housing offers a strong value against high island rents.
You'd rather not manage a high-priced home from afar after you leave.
buy: You expect a long tour or plan to return to or retire in Hawaii.
You can carry Hawaii's high prices and want to build equity with your VA loan.
You're comfortable potentially renting it out at island rates later.
You've found a home and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but high prices and fixed tours make on-base or renting the common choice in Hawaii. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, factor the higher cost of living, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: An island PCS has extra logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Allowance (TLA) for OCONUS-style moves; confirm the current rules.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Get on the housing list early
steps.text: On-base is often the best value against island rents, so contact Island Palm Communities right away while you look off base.

steps.title: Sort your vehicle and shipment
steps.text: Track your sea shipment of household goods and your one shipped vehicle; arrange interim transportation and Hawaii registration.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Hawaii uses one statewide system; confirm your assigned school by address and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at Tripler Army Medical Center or the Schofield clinic.

data.a: The 25th Infantry Division (Tropic Lightning). Schofield is the largest Army installation in Hawaii and the core of U.S. Army Garrison Hawaii, which also includes Wheeler and Fort Shafter.
Yes. A PCS here works like an overseas move (sea shipment, one vehicle, island allowances), though you still receive BAH set by Honolulu County rather than OHA.
On-base via Island Palm Communities is often the best value; off base, Mililani and Wahiawa are close and popular. Expect H-1/H-2 traffic toward Honolulu.
Schofield is MHA HI408 (Honolulu County). Use the calculator above for your grade.

picks.name: Dole Plantation
picks.blurb: A famous pineapple plantation with a giant maze and train, just north of Schofield.

picks.name: North Shore (Haleiwa)
picks.blurb: Oahu's legendary surf coast and laid-back town, a short drive north.

picks.name: Waimea Valley
picks.blurb: A lush botanical valley with a waterfall on the North Shore.

picks.name: Kukaniloko Birthing Stones
picks.blurb: A sacred Hawaiian cultural site on the central plateau near Wahiawa.
